title: Existence of Quasigeodesic Anosov Flows in Hyperbolic 3-Manifolds
speaker: Sergio Fenley
affiliation: Florida State University; Member, School of Mathematics
abstract: A quasigeodesic in a manifold is a curve so that when lifted to the universal cover is uniformly efficient up to a bounded multiplicative and added error in measuring length. A flow is quasigeodesic if all flow lines are quasigeodesics. We prove that an Anosov flow in a closed hyperbolic manifold is quasigeodesic if and only if it is not R-covered. Here R-covered means that the stable 2-dim foliation of the flow, lifts to a foliation in the universal cover whose leaf space is homeomorphic to the real numbers.

proof_stages.label: Anosov and quasigeodesic framework
proof_stages.summary: The talk fixes a closed hyperbolic 3-manifold, defines topological Anosov and pseudo-Anosov flows via stable/unstable foliations, and formulates quasigeodesicity of lifted flow lines using hyperbolic fellow-traveling.

proof_stages.label: R-covered dichotomy and main criterion
proof_stages.summary: R-covered foliations are introduced through the leaf space of the lifted stable or unstable foliation, and the main theorem is stated: an Anosov flow on a closed hyperbolic 3-manifold is quasigeodesic exactly when it is not R-covered.

proof_stages.label: R-covered flows obstruct quasigeodesicity
proof_stages.summary: In the R-covered case, the orbit-space structure forces infinitely many freely homotopic periodic orbits; if the flow were quasigeodesic, their coherent lifts would lie in a bounded hyperbolic tube, forcing stable and unstable leaves to intersect in a way that would put two periodic orbits in one stable leaf, a contradiction.

proof_stages.label: Periodic-orbit boundedness condition P
proof_stages.summary: The proof isolates condition P, a uniform upper bound on the size of pairwise freely homotopic periodic-orbit classes, and uses the bounded-tube obstruction to show quasigeodesicity implies P while invoking the difficult converse theorem that P implies quasigeodesicity.

proof_stages.label: Failure of P forces R-covered topology
proof_stages.summary: Large free-homotopy classes are stacked into annuli, bands, and limiting walls whose projections either form laminations with solid-torus complementary regions or have controlled transverse intersections, and either analysis forces the stable/unstable leaf space to be R-covered.

proof_stages.label: Existence corollary via the criterion
proof_stages.summary: Combining the criterion with Thurston-type results on transverse pseudo-Anosov flows to R-covered foliations shows that any closed hyperbolic 3-manifold admitting an Anosov flow has, possibly after passing to a double cover, a quasigeodesic pseudo-Anosov flow.

proof_stages.label: Boundary-extension and Cannon-Thurston applications
proof_stages.summary: The quasigeodesic endpoint control in the non-R-covered case, together with Thurston's R-covered arguments, gives continuous extension for stable and unstable leaves and supports a canonical pi_1-equivariant sphere-filling map from the compactified orbit-space boundary to the sphere at infinity.
